mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal yields negative forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative returns do not materialize during late-cycle phases when INDPRO declines, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 2.0 percent triggers a restrictive monetary environment that increases discount rates and dampens equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0, or the threshold effect shows no statistical edge over a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor tightening signal fails to predict positive quarterly returns, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity that immediately lift equity sentiment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of cases, or the signal rank correlation with forward returns falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 105 during post-shock recovery phases signals restored manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization that supports corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of post-shock recovery periods, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy drops below 50%.
